targetedTsAreas.orchestration_governance_security_state: bun test src/orchestration/ src/governance/ src/security/ src/state/ -> 442 pass, 0 fail

notes: src/cli/commands/pause.ts exists, imports, and pause tests pass.
Actual CLI writes a checkpoint file, but payload is mostly placeholders: active_task undefined, pending_tasks [], memory_snapshot {}.
Version is hardcoded to 2.3.0 and there is no deferral-tracker or CheckpointSystem integration.
src/cli/commands/continue.ts exists, imports, and continue tests pass.
Actual CLI restores only a summary string; it does not restore memory tier state, context summary, or pending task state into runtime systems.
Staleness/version checks are hardcoded around 2.3.0 and do not use src/context/checkpoint.ts.
workspace-reconstruction, strategy-router, checkpoint, and compression modules all exist and import cleanly.
bun test src/context/ passed (103 pass, 0 fail).
Code includes freshness scoring, decay detection, automatic reconstruction hooks, durability strategy registration, and reconstruction boundary handling in compression.ts.

verdicts.claimed: Society of Thought planning pipeline integration
notes: debate integration modules exist and bun test src/debate/ passed (60 pass, 0 fail).
runPlanningDebate is only referenced in tests/integration files during this audit; there is no evidence of plan-council bundle/runtime wiring.
Debate engine works in isolation, but planning-pipeline integration is not demonstrated in production code.
reliability HUD/gate modules exist and bun test src/reliability/ passed (62 pass, 0 fail).
HUD color/band mapping and history parsing work.
No TypeScript evidence of the claimed session-end reliability report card/evidence export was found.
governance modules exist and bun test src/governance/ passed (48 pass, 0 fail).
preDispatchGovernanceCheck records ledger entries and can deny invalid combinations.
Phase-1 claim said default mode should be advisory, but GovernanceGraphRuntime defaults to soft-block now; collusion detection itself only warns unless hard-block mode is used.
memory tier-status command exists, imports, tests pass, and CLI prints JSON/table output.
The command only reads .omg/state/memory-tier-status.json or tier-status.json snapshots.
src/state/memory-store.ts has no Auto/Micro/Ship routing, so this command is not backed by a real TS CMMS implementation.
session-lifecycle/session modules exist and session-lifecycle tests pass.
Periodic freshness checking, context-decay-detected events, and reconstruction attempts are implemented.
Task claim also required /pause checkpoint freshness integration, but pause.ts does not capture freshness state.
src/phase1.integration.test.ts exists and passes (8 pass, 0 fail).
The tests cover checkpoint metadata, debate invocation, and governance ledger behavior.
They do not verify real CMMS tiers, real /pause+/continue command flow, or handoff retry demotion behavior claimed in the roadmap.

verdicts.claimed: planning context retention via CMMS Ship tier
notes: planning/context-retention.ts exists and tests pass.
Persistence works across store reinitialization.
Implementation is a file-backed planning-context store that labels records tier:'ship'; it does not integrate with a real TS Ship tier because src/state/memory-store.ts has no tier routing.
router.ts exists/imports and router tests pass.
Router selects codex/gemini/ccg from keyword heuristics.
There is no budget-tracker integration or explicit hard-subtask escalation evidence in router.ts; task-level escalation cost tracking exists only in separate auto-escalation.ts helpers.
wave-optimizer.ts exists/imports and tests pass.
Implementation performs topological grouping into waves and validates dependency ordering/cycles.
Behavior matches the core roadmap claim for automatic wave computation.
qa-generator.ts exists/imports and tests pass.
Generator emits happy-path and error-case scenarios with tool selection heuristics and markdown formatting.
While heuristic, the deliverable exists and works as a concrete generator.
traceability helper module and tests exist.
Current TS implementation is utility-only: parse/format/generate report from supplied data.
No evidence of actual git commit integration or phase-gate traceability report generation was found in TypeScript runtime code.
CLI command exists/tests pass and actual CLI produces a markdown report file.
Runtime/autoresearch_engine.py only does local grep-based code/file search; no web-source section is emitted and TS CLI ignores --max-tokens.
The deliverable works in a reduced form, not at the full roadmap scope (web + filesystem + code search + budget envelope).

verdicts.claimed: smart task handling auto-escalation to stronger models
notes: auto-escalation.ts exists/imports and tests pass.
Decision helper supports user override and cost tracking in isolation.
It is not wired into router.ts or broader task-routing flow, so the roadmap integration claim is incomplete.
Python runtime has DaemonConfig/start_daemon/stop_daemon helpers.
TypeScript CLI has no --daemon or --stop flag support; running omg autoresearch topic --daemon fails with Unknown argument: daemon.
The TS-facing deliverable therefore exists only as backend scaffolding.
domain-packs/validator.ts and phase4 integration tests exist and pass.
Current validator checks required files/directories and computes a simple issue-count score.
It does not generate projects, run npm installs/builds/tests, or validate actual pack output behavior as claimed.
soft-block tests exist and pass; GovernanceGraphRuntime defaults to soft-block.
However detectCollusion returns warn in soft-block mode and blocks only in hard-block mode.
That means collusion-specific soft-block enforcement/override behavior is not fully implemented as claimed.
tests/integration/test_aos_e2e.ts exists and passes when run directly with bun test ./tests/integration/test_aos_e2e.ts.
The test mostly checks imports/constants and synthetic behavior, not full cross-feature orchestration.
src/harness/harness-runner.ts is only referenced by its own tests and uses fake passing/failing runners, so the claimed working harness runner/orchestrated AOS flow is not present.

cmms: src/state/memory-store.ts is a single SQLite-backed namespace store with no Auto/Micro/Ship tier model.
tier-status CLI reads snapshot JSON instead of querying a tiered TS store.
providers: Adapters only implement availability/auth health checks and MCP config generation; no prompt execution/API-call surface was found in these TS adapters.
harness: src/harness/harness-runner.ts is schema/aggregation scaffolding with synthetic helper runners.
No production wiring to actual agent orchestration or multi-layer test execution was found.
